title: Executive Order No. 99, s. 2025
text:
MALACANAN PALACE
MANILA

BY THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES

EXECUTIVE ORDERNO. 99

ESTABLISHING THE PRESIDENTIAL FILIPINNOVATION AWARDS

WHEREAS, Section 2 of Republic Act (RA) No. 11293 or the “Philippine
Innovation Act,” declares it a policy of the State to foster innovation as a vital
component of national development and sustainable economic growth;

WHEREAS, Section 5 of RA No. 11293 provides that while the country’s
innovation goals shall be directed at developing new technologies, it shall likewise
seek to harness global knowledge and technology that will aid in developing new
processes or services for increasing productivity and promoting overall public welfare;

WHEREAS, under Section 20 of RA No. 11293, the National Innovation Council
(NIC), in cooperation with various concerned agencies, shall undertake an information
and community education program to rally everyone around the country’s innovation
agenda and priorities and to generate public support for these initiatives;

WHEREAS, the NIC, through Resolution No. 1 (s. 2024), resolved to approve
the conduct of the National Regional Innovation Awards and Competition (NRIAC) or
the “Filipinnovation Awards,” an annual competition envisioned to be the most
prestigious innovation competition in the country, where the winners will be awarded
by the President;

WHEREAS, consistent with the objectives set forth in RA No. 11293, the
Filipinnovation Awards aims to, among others: (a) bring out the best Filipino innovators
and allow them to demonstrate their talents; (b) serve as a platform to connect
innovators with industry stakeholders and potential partners, including local and
foreign investors; and (c) foster a more robust innovation ecosystem in the country;

WHEREAS, Section 7 of Executive Order No. 236 (s. 2003) provides that the
President may confer Presidential Awards not otherwise included in the Order of
Precedence of the Honors Code; and

WHEREAS, Section 17, Article VII of the Constitution vests in the President the

power of control over all Executive departments, bureaus, and offices, and the
mandate to ensure the faithful execution of laws;

THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES
 
NOW, THEREFORE, |, FERDINAND R. MARCOS, JR., President of the
Philippines, by virtue of the powers vested in me by the Constitution and existing laws,
do hereby order:

Section 1. Presidential Filipinnovation Awards. The NRIAC or the
Filipinnovation Awards, which shall now be known as the “Presidential Filipinnovation
Awards,” is an annual competition that aims to recognize enterprises and individuals
with innovative products or services that are Filipino-owned and -registered, and
currently at early-stage commercialization. The Presidential Filipinnovation Awards
shall highlight local talent in order to amplify the impact of innovations in the country’s
economic development.

Section 2. Conferment of the Award. The Presidential Filipinnovation Award
shall be conferred by the President or the Executive Secretary, “By Authority of the
President.”

The Presidential Filipinnovation Award shall be in the form of a medallion and
plaque containing the President’s seal, citation, and signature. The recipients of the
Award shall also receive a grant and/or support, which may be in the form of
mentorship, coaching, or participation in international events, to assist them in
showcasing their innovations.

Section 3. Selection Committee and Board of Judges. The NIC shall
constitute a Selection Committee that shall administer the annual search for and lead
the selection of the members of the national and sub-national Board of Judges. The
Board of Judges shall be tasked to review and assess entries in the Presidential
Filipinnovation Awards, provide feedback, and score said entries based on the criteria
provided by the NIC.

The NIC, the Selection Committee, as well as the national and sub-national
Board of Judges, shall ensure transparency and fairness in the conduct of all activities
and processes related to the Presidential Filipinnovation Awards.

Section 4. Implementing Guidelines. Within fifteen (15) days from the
effectivity of this Order, the NIC shall formulate and issue the necessary guidelines for
the effective implementation of this Order. Said guidelines shall include, among others,
the mechanics, stages, and processes for the conduct of the Presidential
Filipinnovation Awards, criteria for the selection and the corresponding awards and
incentives for the recipients of the Presidential Filipinnovation Award, as well as duties
and functions of Selection Committee and Board of Judges.

Section 5. Funding. The initial funding requirements necessary for the
implementation of this Order shall be charged against the respective appropriations of
the member-agencies of the NIC. Thereafter, the funding requirements necessary for
the continued implementation of this Order shall be included in the budget proposal of
said agencies, subject to the usual budget preparation process.

Section 6. Separability. If any part or provision of this Order is held invalid or
unconstitutional, the provisions not affected thereby shall remain in full force and
effect.
 
Section 7. Repeal. All orders, rules and regulations, and issuances or parts
thereof inconsistent with this Order are hereby repealed, amended, or modified
accordingly.

Section 8. Effectivity. This Order shall take effect immediately upon
publication in the Official Gazette or a newspaper of general circulation.

DONE, in the City of Manila, this 6th day of October, inthe
Year of our Lord, Two Thousand and Twenty-Five.

 

By the President:

  

of the President
G RECORDS OFFICE

TED COPY

  
  

REP
